title: T-049 — quality_complaints schema + RLS (P1 stub, 09-e-01)
prompt:
# T-049 — quality_complaints schema + RLS (P1 stub)

PRD: §6.3 quality_complaints DDL (stub P1, full P2 EPIC 8M), §9.1 RLS, §11.6 V-QA-COMPLAINT-001/002
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create quality_complaints stub table (CRUD + link to NCR) per §6.3 with received_via channel taxonomy, severity-driven response_due_at, 7y retention, RLS function-form. Full RCA/CAPA workflow is P2 EPIC 8M.

## Implementation contract
Migration 0142 emits CREATE SEQUENCE quality_complaint_seq + CREATE TABLE quality_complaints per §6.3 verbatim (complaint_number GENERATED, received_via CHECK in {email,phone,portal,retailer_system,other}, severity CHECK, status CHECK 6 values, linked_ncr_id FK, retention_until 7y GENERATED). idx_complaint_open partial. RLS function-form. Drizzle schema export. response_due_at logic: if severity='critical', received + 2 business days; otherwise NULL (server-side enforcement of V-QA-COMPLAINT-002 in T-053 Server Action).

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0142_quality_complaints.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/qualityComplaints.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/quality-complaints-schema.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration 0142 applied, when \d quality_complaints runs, then all §6.3 columns exist (received_via CHECK 5 values, status CHECK 6 values, retention_until 7y GENERATED) and RLS function-form is enabled
2. Given the partial idx_complaint_open index, when EXPLAIN ANALYZE the open-complaints query runs, then the partial index is selected
3. Given two tenants with app.current_org_id() each, when each inserts and SELECTs, then each sees only its own rows

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: quality-complaints-schema.test.ts before migration
- Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test quality-complaints-schema'

## Risk red lines
- P1 is STUB (CRUD + NCR link). Do not build full RCA/CAPA — P2 EPIC 8M.
- Use app.current_org_id() function (Wave0)
- 7y retention required (BRCGS §3.11.1)
- received_via CHECK must include all 5 channels per §6.3

pipeline_inputs.description: quality_complaints stub schema (CRUD + NCR link) + RLS function-form + 7y retention + idx_complaint_open partial. Full workflow P2 EPIC 8M.
pipeline_inputs.details: Migration 0142 + Drizzle schema per §6.3 verbatim. complaint_number GENERATED via sequence. received_via CHECK in 5 channels {email, phone, portal, retailer_system, other} — channels are tenant-configurable per ADR-034 (label only; the underlying enum stays). status CHECK 6 values {received, under_review, investigating, resolved, closed, rejected}. retention_until 7y GENERATED per BRCGS §3.11.1. idx_complaint_open partial WHERE status NOT IN ('resolved','closed','rejected'). RLS function-form. PRD anchors verified: §6.3 source DDL; §9.1 RLS pattern; §11.6 V-QA-COMPLAINT-001/002 (received_at validation + critical 2-day response — deferred to T-053 Server Action).
out_of_scope: Do not implement complaint Server Actions (T-053)
Do not implement RCA/CAPA workflow (P2 EPIC 8M)
Do not implement complaint_categories reference table (deferred)
acceptance_criteria: Given migration 0142 applied, when \d quality_complaints runs, then all §6.3 columns exist (received_via CHECK 5 values, status CHECK 6 values, retention_until 7y GENERATED) and RLS function-form is enabled
Given the partial idx_complaint_open index, when EXPLAIN ANALYZE the open-complaints query runs, then the partial index is selected
Given two tenants with app.current_org_id() each, when each inserts and SELECTs, then each sees only its own rows
